data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0104-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — run 0010 T12 (Picard representability cone)

- Useful context: run 0010's final T12 Horizon session (`0095-horizon-T12`) failed at harness startup with an empty diff — the productive T12 output landed earlier. Real, verified work this run: `Grassmannian.representable` (`Picard/GrassmannianRepresentability.lean`, modulo two descent leaves) and `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (`Picard/QuotScheme.lean`, axiom-clean). Durable recipes in memory `I-0109` / `I-0113` / `I-0120`.

- Relevant files for the open frontier: `Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean` (`Scheme.QuotFunctor`, `Scheme.Grassmannian` are real functors), `Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean` (`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` real, axiom-clean), `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (residual sorries are the FBC-gated base-change leaves plus the `QuotScheme`/`Grassmannian.representable` endgames). Blueprint chapter: `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`.

- Consistency concern to keep in view: `I-0118` — the pinned `Scheme.QuotScheme` hypothesizes only `IsProper`+`LocallyOfFiniteType`, but Nitsure Sec. 5 needs projective / relatively very ample / coherent; the statement is false-or-open as pinned (Hironaka-type counterexample), not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old `Grassmannian.representable` skeleton that was corrected this run.

- Adjacent live legs: `AJC.fbc` still has 3 leaf sorries in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` (two cosimplicial `naturality` fields + `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits`, route analysis in `I-0076`/`I-0083`); `AJC.albanese` T9 port is fully scoped but unstarted (`I-0112`/`I-0115`), with the SUB-ahead support files (`PolePurity`, `SmoothPrimeRegularity`, `StandardSmoothDimension`) already present under AJC `Albanese/`.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

Now that most of the necessary architecture is already made, `GrassmannianQuot`, `FlatteningStratification`, `GenericFlatnessGeometric`, etc, you should already commit to the hard sorries and close the representability task.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
(empty)

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

10	/-!
11	# Representability of the relative Grassmannian (`thm:grassmannian_representable`)
12	
13	This file states and attacks the representability of the relative Grassmannian
14	functor `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1` (`Scheme.Grassmannian`,
15	`QuotFunctorDef.lean`) by an `S`-scheme, for `V` a locally free
16	`O_S`-module of rank `r` and `1 ≤ d ≤ r` ([Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2);
17	blueprint `thm:grassmannian_representable`).
18	
19	The statement here **corrects** the earlier skeleton of `QuotFunctorDef.lean`,
20	which quantified over an arbitrary sheaf of modules `V` with no rank data:
21	that form is not the [Nitsure] theorem (whose hypotheses are `V` locally free
22	of rank `r ≥ d ≥ 1`) and is not known to be true for non-quasi-coherent `V`.
23	The universe is pinned to `Scheme.{0}`: the merged absolute chart construction
24	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme` / `.represents`,
25	`GrassmannianQuot.lean`) lives over the genuinely terminal `Spec ℤ` of
26	`Scheme.{0}`, and the relative theorem is obtained from it.
27	
28	## Contents
29	
30	* `Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.congrModule` / `Scheme.Grassmannian.congrIso` —
31	  transport of the Grassmannian functor along an isomorphism `V ≅ V'` of the
32	  classified module (pure functoriality, no pseudofunctor coherence).
33	* `Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.toRankQuotient` / `.ofRankQuotient` /
34	  `Scheme.Grassmannian.freeCompare` — for the free module `V = O_S^r`, the
35	  comparison `Grass(O_S^r, d) ≅ (Over.forget S)ᵒᵖ ⋙ Grass(r, d)` with the
36	  merged absolute Grassmannian functor, re-presenting the pulled-back free
37	  module through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso`.  Naturality is the free
38	  coherence `pullbackFreeIso_comp` of `GlueDescent.lean`.
39	* `Scheme.Grassmannian.prodRepresentableBy` — the absolute Grassmannian,
40	  pulled back to `Over S` along the forgetful functor, is representable by
41	  the product `S ⨯ Gr(d, r)` with its first projection (`Spec ℤ` is terminal
42	  in `Scheme.{0}`, so the product is the base change of `Gr(d, r)` to `S`).
43	* `Scheme.Grassmannian.representable_of_iso_free` — representability of
44	  `Grass(V, d)` for globally trivialised `V ≅ O_S^r` (complete proof).
45	* `Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` — the [Nitsure] statement, for `V`
46	  locally free of rank `r`: glue `representable_of_iso_free` over a
47	  trivialising cover of `(S, V)`.
48

51	Blueprint: `thm:grassmannian_representable`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`
52	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
53	Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020).
54	-/

383	end Grassmannian
384	
385	/-! ## §5. Zariski-local presheaves on `Sch/S` and descent of representability
386	
387	The remaining step from the trivialised case to a locally free `V` is
388	*Zariski descent of representability* (EGA 0_I 4.5.4): a presheaf on `Sch/S`
389	satisfying the Zariski sheaf axiom that is representable over every member of
390	an open cover of `S` is representable.  We encode the sheaf axiom concretely:
391	for every `T : Over S` and every open cover of the total space `T.left`,
392	compatible local sections of `F` glue uniquely. -/
393	
394	/-- The restriction of `T : Over S` to an open `W ⊆ T.left`, as an object of
395	`Over S`. -/
396	noncomputable def overRes {S : Scheme.{0}} (T : Over S) (W : T.left.Opens) :
397	    Over S :=
398	  Over.mk (W.ι ≫ T.hom)
399	
400	/-- The restriction morphism `T|_W ⟶ T` in `Over S`. -/
401	noncomputable def overResHom {S : Scheme.{0}} (T : Over S) (W : T.left.Opens) :
402	    overRes T W ⟶ T :=
403	  Over.homMk W.ι rfl
404	
405	/-- The inclusion `T|_{W'} ⟶ T|_W` in `Over S` attached to `W' ≤ W`. -/
406	noncomputable def overResLE {S : Scheme.{0}} (T : Over S)
407	    {W' W : T.left.Opens} (h : W' ≤ W) : overRes T W' ⟶ overRes T W :=
408	  Over.homMk (T.left.homOfLE h)
409	    ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
410	      (congrArg (· ≫ T.hom) (T.left.homOfLE_ι h)))
411	
412	/-- **The Zariski sheaf axiom for a presheaf on `Sch/S`**: for every
413	`T : Over S` and every open cover `{W k}` of the total space `T.left`, a
414	family of sections of `F` over the restrictions `T|_{W k}` that agree on the
415	pairwise intersections glues to a unique section over `T`.  This is the sheaf
416	condition for the (big) Zariski site of `S`, stated concretely against open
417	covers of the total space; representable presheaves satisfy it, and it is the
418	hypothesis under which local representability descends
419	(`Scheme.representable_of_openCover`). -/
420	def IsZariskiSheafOver {S : Scheme.{0}} (F : (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1) : Prop :=
421	  ∀ (T : Over S) {κ : Type} (W : κ → T.left.Opens), (⨆ k, W k = ⊤) →
422	    ∀ x : ∀ k, F.obj (Opposite.op (overRes T (W k))),
423	    (∀ k l : κ, F.map (overResLE T (inf_le_left : W k ⊓ W l ≤ W k)).op (x k)
424	      = F.map (overResLE T (inf_le_right : W k ⊓ W l ≤ W l)).op (x l)) →
425	    ∃! x₀ : F.obj (Opposite.op T), ∀ k, F.map (overResHom T (W k)).op x₀ = x k
426	
427	/-- **Zariski descent of representability** (EGA 0_I 4.5.4; cf. Stacks 01JJ):
428	a Zariski-local presheaf `F` on `Sch/S` that is representable over every
429	member of an open cover of `S` is representable by an `S`-scheme.
430	
431	Proof route (typed `sorry`, the remaining leaf of
432	`thm:grassmannian_representable`): let `Y_i → U_i` represent the restrictions
433	`F|_{Sch/U_i}`.  Over `U_i ∩ U_j` both `Y_i` and `Y_j` restrict to
434	representing objects of `F|_{Sch/(U_i ∩ U_j)}`, so they are canonically
435	isomorphic (uniqueness of representing objects), and the canonical isos
436	satisfy the cocycle condition by uniqueness again; glue the `Y_i` along them
437	(`Scheme.GlueData`) to a scheme `Y → S`.  For `T : Over S`, a morphism
438	`T ⟶ Y` restricts over the preimages of the `U_i` to morphisms into the
439	`Y_i`, i.e. to compatible local sections of `F`, which glue by the sheaf
440	axiom; conversely a section of `F` over `T` restricts to local sections,
441	whose classifying morphisms into the `Y_i` agree on overlaps and glue
442	(`Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphisms`) to `T ⟶ Y`.  The two constructions are
443	mutually inverse by locality of morphism equality and the uniqueness half of
444	the sheaf axiom. -/
445	theorem representable_of_openCover {S : Scheme.{0}} (F : (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1)
446	    (hF : IsZariskiSheafOver F) {ι : Type} (U : ι → S.Opens)
447	    (hU : ⨆ i, U i = ⊤)
448	    (hloc : ∀ i, ∃ Y : Over (U i).toScheme,
449	      Nonempty (((Over.map (U i).ι).op ⋙ F).RepresentableBy Y)) :
450	    ∃ Y : Over S, Nonempty (F.RepresentableBy Y) := by
451	  sorry
452	
453	/-! ## §6. Restriction of the Grassmannian to an open of the base
454	
455	For a base morphism `j : S' ⟶ S`, restricting the relative Grassmannian
456	functor along `Over.map j : Over S' ⥤ Over S` classifies quotients of the
457	restricted module: the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp` re-presents a
458	quotient of `(T.hom ≫ j)^* V` as a quotient of `T.hom^* (j^* V)`, naturally
459	in `T` (`restrictIso`).  Combined with the trivialised case this yields local
460	representability over each member of a trivialising cover of `(S, V)`
461	(`representable_restrict`), and Zariski descent closes the theorem. -/
462	
463	namespace Grassmannian
464	
465	variable {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
466	
467	/-- Restriction of a quotient family along a base morphism `j : S' ⟶ S`: a
468	family over `(Over.map j).obj T` classifies quotients of `(T.hom ≫ j)^* V`,
469	which the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp` re-presents as a quotient
470	of `T.hom^* (j^* V)`. -/
471	noncomputable def restrictBase {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules}
472	    {d : ℕ} {T : Over S'}
473	    (x : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient V d ((Over.map j).obj T)) :
474	    Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T where
475	  F := x.F
476	  q := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q
477	  epi :=
478	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
479	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom inferInstance
480	      x.q x.epi
481	  locFree := x.locFree
482	
483	/-- Inverse of `restrictBase`: a quotient of `T.hom^* (j^* V)` re-presented
484	as a family over `(Over.map j).obj T`. -/
485	noncomputable def corestrictBase {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules}
486	    {d : ℕ} {T : Over S'}
487	    (y : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T) :
488	    Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient V d ((Over.map j).obj T) where
489	  F := y.F
490	  q := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ y.q
491	  epi :=
492	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
493	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv inferInstance
494	      y.q y.epi
495	  locFree := y.locFree
496	
497	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
498	/-- `restrictBase` respects the equivalence of families. -/
499	lemma restrictBase_rel {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules} {d : ℕ}
500	    {T : Over S'} {x y : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient V d ((Over.map j).obj T)}
501	    (h : x.Rel y) : (restrictBase j x).Rel (restrictBase j y) := by
502	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
503	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
504	  change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
505	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ y.q
506	  rw [Category.assoc]
507	  exact congrArg (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ ·) hf
508	
509	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
510	/-- `corestrictBase` respects the equivalence of families. -/
511	lemma corestrictBase_rel {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S) {V : S.Modules} {d : ℕ}
512	    {T : Over S'}
513	    {x y : Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T}
514	    (h : x.Rel y) : (corestrictBase j x).Rel (corestrictBase j y) := by
515	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
516	  refine ⟨f, ?_⟩
517	  change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ x.q) ≫ f.hom
518	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ y.q
519	  rw [Category.assoc]
520	  exact congrArg (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ ·) hf
521	
522	/-- The value-level comparison between the Grassmannian of `V` over the image
523	of `T : Over S'` and the Grassmannian of the restricted module `j^* V`. -/
524	noncomputable def restrictEquiv {S' : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S']
525	    (j : S' ⟶ S) (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) (T : Over S') :
526	    Quotient (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid V d ((Over.map j).obj T)) ≃
527	    Quotient (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid
528	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d T) where
529	  toFun := Quotient.map (restrictBase j) (fun _ _ h => restrictBase_rel j h)
530	  invFun := Quotient.map (corestrictBase j) (fun _ _ h => corestrictBase_rel j h)
531	  left_inv := fun z => by
532	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
533	    | _ x =>
534	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
535	      change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫
536	          (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q)) ≫ 𝟙 _
537	        = x.q
538	      rw [Category.comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
539	  right_inv := fun z => by
540	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
541	    | _ x =>
542	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
543	      change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom ≫
544	          (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).inv ≫ x.q)) ≫ 𝟙 _
545	        = x.q
546	      rw [Category.comp_id, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
547	
548	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
549	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] in
550	/-- **Pseudofunctor coherence for the base restriction** — the naturality
551	core of `restrictIso`: the `pullbackComp` re-presentation commutes with the
552	pullback actions of the two Grassmannian functors.  An instance of
553	`Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence` after normalising the
554	`pullbackCongr` casts to `eqToHom`s. -/
555	lemma restrictBase_naturality_core {S' : Scheme.{0}} (j : S' ⟶ S)
556	    (V : S.Modules) {T T' : Over S'} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) :
557	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T'.hom j).app V).hom ≫
558	      (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ((Over.map j).map ψ)) V).inv
559	    = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V)).inv ≫
560	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.left).map
561	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.hom j).app V).hom := by
562	  have h₃ : ψ.left ≫ (T.hom ≫ j) = T'.hom ≫ j := by
563	    rw [← Category.assoc, Over.w ψ]
564	  have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence ψ.left T.hom
565	    (Over.w ψ).symm j rfl rfl h₃ V
566	  -- move to the `hom`-form `map compT ≫ tri_ψ'.hom = tri_ψ.hom ≫ compT'`
567	  rw [eq_comm, Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
568	  -- expand the triangle isos and normalise all casts to `eqToHom`
569	  simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.app_hom, Category.assoc,
570	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_hom_app, Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr_inv_app,
571	    eqToHom_refl, Category.comp_id] at key ⊢
572	  exact key.symm
573	
574	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
575	/-- **Restriction comparison for the Grassmannian**: for a base morphism
576	`j : S' ⟶ S`, the relative Grassmannian of `V`, restricted along
577	`Over.map j`, is naturally isomorphic to the relative Grassmannian of the
578	restricted module `j^* V` over `S'`. -/
579	noncomputable def restrictIso {S' : Scheme.{0}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S']
580	    (j : S' ⟶ S) (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
581	    (Over.map j).op ⋙ Scheme.Grassmannian V d ≅
582	      Scheme.Grassmannian ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V) d :=
583	  NatIso.ofComponents
584	    (fun T => Equiv.toIso (restrictEquiv j V d T.unop))
585	    (fun {T T'} ψ => by
586	      ext z
587	      induction z using Quotient.ind with
588	      | _ x =>
589	        change Quotient.mk _ (restrictBase j
590	            (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ((Over.map j).map ψ.unop) x))
591	          = Quotient.mk _ (Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong ψ.unop
592	            (restrictBase j x))
593	        refine Quotient.sound ⟨Iso.refl _, ?_⟩
594	        change (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T'.unop.hom j).app V).hom ≫
595	            (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ((Over.map j).map ψ.unop)) V).inv ≫
596	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q) ≫ 𝟙 _
597	          = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w ψ.unop)
598	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj V)).inv ≫
599	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map
600	              (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp T.unop.hom j).app V).hom ≫ x.q)
601	        rw [Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
602	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
603	        exact congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ.unop.left).map x.q)
604	          (restrictBase_naturality_core j V ψ.unop))
605	
606	/-- **Local representability over a trivialising open**: over an open
607	`U ⊆ S` on which `V` trivialises, the restriction of the Grassmannian
608	functor to `Sch/U` is representable.  Complete proof: compose the
609	restriction comparison `restrictIso` with the trivialised case
610	`representable_of_iso_free` (the trivialisation index is re-normalised from
611	`ULift (Fin r)` to `Fin r` through the free functor). -/
612	theorem representable_restrict {V : S.Modules} {r : ℕ} (U : S.Opens)
613	    (e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj V ≅
614	      SheafOfModules.free (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{0} (Fin r)))
615	    {d : ℕ} (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
616	    ∃ Y : Over U.toScheme,
617	      Nonempty (((Over.map U.ι).op ⋙ Scheme.Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
618	  obtain ⟨Y, ⟨hY⟩⟩ := representable_of_iso_free
619	    (e ≪≫ (SheafOfModules.freeFunctor (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)).mapIso
620	      (Equiv.toIso Equiv.ulift)) hd hdr
621	  exact ⟨Y, ⟨hY.ofIso (restrictIso U.ι V d).symm⟩⟩
622	
623	/-- **The Grassmannian functor is a Zariski sheaf** ([Nitsure] §1; the
624	implicit locality behind the chart construction).  Families of rank-`d`
625	locally free quotients glue along an open cover of the parameter scheme:
626	the target sheaves glue by descent of sheaves of modules along the cover
627	(the `Scheme.Modules.glue` engine of `GlueDescent.lean` at the glue data of
628	the cover), the quotient maps glue by the sheaf property of `Hom`, local
629	freeness and epi-ness are local, and the glued family is unique up to the
630	equivalence `ker q = ker q'` because an isomorphism of quotients commuting
631	with the epimorphisms is unique when it exists, so the local comparison isos
632	agree on overlaps and glue.  [typed `sorry` — the second remaining leaf of
633	`thm:grassmannian_representable`.] -/
634	theorem isZariskiSheaf (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
635	    IsZariskiSheafOver (Scheme.Grassmannian V d) := by
636	  sorry
637	
638	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian** (`thm:grassmannian_representable`,
639	[Nitsure] §1 "Construction of Grassmannian", Exercise (2)): for a locally
640	noetherian `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`, and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`,
641	the functor `Grass(V, d)` is representable by an `S`-scheme.  Proof: `V`
642	trivialises on an open cover of `S`; over each member the functor is
643	representable (`representable_restrict`, via the merged absolute chart
644	construction), and representability descends along the cover
645	(`Scheme.representable_of_openCover`) since the Grassmannian functor is a
646	Zariski sheaf (`isZariskiSheaf`). -/
647	theorem representable {V : S.Modules} {r : ℕ}
648	    (hV : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank V r) {d : ℕ}
649	    (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
650	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Scheme.Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
651	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hU, hloc⟩ := hV
652	  refine representable_of_openCover (Scheme.Grassmannian V d)
653	    (isZariskiSheaf V d) U hU (fun i => ?_)
654	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
655	  exact representable_restrict (U i) e hd hdr
656	
657	end Grassmannian
658	
659	end Scheme

module

public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.Representable
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Sites.BigZariski
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.GluingOneHypercover
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyBijective
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Pullback.Iso

/-!
# Representability of schemes is a local property

In this file we prove that a sheaf of types `F` on `Sch` is representable if it is
locally representable.

## Main result
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LocalRepresentability.isRepresentable`:
  Suppose
  * F is a `Type u`-valued sheaf on `Sch` with respect to the Zariski topology
  * X : ι → Sch is a family of schemes
  * f : Π i, yoneda.obj (X i) ⟶ F is a family of relatively representable open immersions
  * f is jointly surjective

  Then `F` is representable.

## References
* https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01JJ

-/

@[expose] public section

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory Category Limits Opposite

universe u

namespace Scheme

/-
Consider the following setup:
* F is `Type u`-valued a sheaf on `Sch` with respect to the Zariski topology
* X : ι → Sch is a family of schemes
* f : Π i, yoneda.obj (X i) ⟶ F is a family of relatively representable open immersions

Later, we will also assume:
* The family f is locally surjective with respect to the Zariski topology
-/
variable (F : Sheaf Scheme.zariskiTopology.{u} (Type u))
  {ι : Type u} {X : ι → Scheme.{u}}
  (f : (i : ι) → yoneda.obj (X i) ⟶ F.1) (hf : ∀ i, IsOpenImmersion.presheaf (f i))

namespace LocalRepresentability

variable {F f} (i j k : ι)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open Functor.relativelyRepresentable in
/-- We get a family of gluing data by taking `U i = X i` and `V i j = (hf i).rep.pullback (f j)`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def glueData : GlueData where
  J := ι
  U := X
  V := fun (i, j) ↦ (hf i).rep.pullback (f j)
  f i j := (hf i).rep.fst' (f j)
  f_mono i j :=
    have := (hf j).property _ _ _ ((hf i).1.isPullback' (f j)).flip
    IsOpenImmersion.mono _
  f_id i := IsOpenImmersion.isIso_fst'_self IsOpenImmersion.le_monomorphisms (hf i)
  t i j := (hf i).rep.symmetry (hf j).rep

    simp [IsOpenImmersion.fst'_self_eq_snd IsOpenImmersion.le_monomorphisms (hf i)]

/-!
# The big Zariski site of schemes

In this file, we define the Zariski topology, as a Grothendieck topology on the
category `Scheme.{u}`: this is `Scheme.zariskiTopology.{u}`. If `X : Scheme.{u}`,
the Zariski topology on `Over X` can be obtained as `Scheme.zariskiTopology.over X`
(see `CategoryTheory.Sites.Over`.).

TODO:
* If `Y : Scheme.{u}`, define a continuous functor from the category of opens of `Y`
  to `Over Y`, and show that a presheaf on `Over Y` is a sheaf for the Zariski topology
  iff its "restriction" to the topological space `Z` is a sheaf for all `Z : Over Y`.
* We should have good notions of (pre)sheaves of `Type (u + 1)` (e.g. associated
  sheaf functor, pushforward, pullbacks) on `Scheme.{u}` for this topology. However,
  some constructions in the `CategoryTheory.Sites` folder currently assume that
  the site is a small category: this should be generalized. As a result,
  this big Zariski site can considered as a test case of the Grothendieck topology API
  for future applications to étale cohomology.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v u

open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

/-- The Zariski pretopology on the category of schemes. -/
def zariskiPretopology : Pretopology Scheme.{u} :=
  pretopology @IsOpenImmersion

/-- The Zariski topology on the category of schemes. -/
abbrev zariskiTopology : GrothendieckTopology Scheme.{u} :=
  grothendieckTopology IsOpenImmersion

lemma zariskiTopology_eq : zariskiTopology.{u} = zariskiPretopology.toGrothendieck :=
  Precoverage.toGrothendieck_toPretopology_eq_toGrothendieck.symm

instance subcanonical_zariskiTopology : zariskiTopology.Subcanonical := by
  apply GrothendieckTopology.Subcanonical.of_isSheaf_yoneda_obj
  intro X
  rw [Precoverage.isSheaf_toGrothendieck_iff_of_isStableUnderBaseChange]
  rintro Y S hS x hx
  obtain ⟨(𝓤 : OpenCover Y), rfl⟩ := exists_cover_of_mem_pretopology hS
  let e : Y ⟶ X := 𝓤.glueMorphisms (fun j => x (𝓤.f _) (.mk _)) <| by
    intro i j
    apply hx
    exact Limits.pullback.condition
  refine ⟨e, ?_, ?_⟩
  · rintro Z e ⟨j⟩
    dsimp [e]
    rw [𝓤.ι_glueMorphisms]
  · intro e' h
    apply 𝓤.hom_ext

    exact h (𝓤.f j) (.mk j)

/-- A morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` in `D` is said to be relatively representable if for any
`g : F.obj a ⟶ Y`, there exists a pullback square of the following form

F.obj b --F.map snd--> F.obj a

    X ------- f --------> Y

open Functor.relativelyRepresentable

variable {X Y : D} (P : MorphismProperty C)

/-- Given a morphism property `P` in a category `C`, a functor `F : C ⥤ D` and a morphism
`f : X ⟶ Y` in `D`. Then `f` satisfies the morphism property `P.relative` with respect to `F` iff:
* The morphism is representable with respect to `F`
* For any morphism `g : F.obj a ⟶ Y`, the property `P` holds for any represented pullback of
  `f` by `g`. -/
def relative : MorphismProperty D :=
  fun X Y f ↦ F.relativelyRepresentable f ∧
    ∀ ⦃a b : C⦄ (g : F.obj a ⟶ Y) (fst : F.obj b ⟶ X) (snd : b ⟶ a)
      (_ : IsPullback fst (F.map snd) f g), P snd

/-- Given a morphism property `P` in a category `C`, a morphism `f : F ⟶ G` of presheaves in the
category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type v` satisfies the morphism property `P.presheaf` iff:
* The morphism is representable.
* For any morphism `g : F.obj a ⟶ G`, the property `P` holds for any represented pullback of
  `f` by `g`.

This is implemented as a special case of the more general notion of `P.relative`, to the case when
the functor `F` is `yoneda`. -/
abbrev presheaf : MorphismProperty (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type v₁) := P.relative yoneda

variable {P} {F}

/-- A morphism satisfying `P.relative` is representable. -/
lemma relative.rep {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : P.relative F f) : F.relativelyRepresentable f :=
  hf.1

lemma relative.property {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : P.relative F f) :
    ∀ ⦃a b : C⦄ (g : F.obj a ⟶ Y) (fst : F.obj b ⟶ X) (snd : b ⟶ a)
    (_ : IsPullback fst (F.map snd) f g), P snd :=
  hf.2
